articles.title: CLW-Bulk Randomize Answer Choices
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document the steps to bulk randomize the answer choices in D2L multiple choice or multiple response questions. PROCEDUREÂ . After adding the questions to your Qâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document the steps to bulk randomize the answer choices in D2L multiple choice or multiple response questions. PROCEDUREÂ . After adding the questions to your Qâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the steps to bulk randomize the answer choices in D2L multiple choice or multiple response questions.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. After adding the questions to your Question Library, go to Course Admin and Import / Export / Copy ComponentsÂ 

  1. Choose Export as Brightspace Package and click StartÂ 
  1. Â Check the box next to Question Library, choose Select individual items to export, and click ContinueÂ 

  1. Check the section or questions to export and click Continue
  2. Â Click Export and download the zip file when it is readyÂ (**Note: There will only be one zip file created - even if you select multiple collections in the step above)

Â **You will get a notification when export is complete. Clicking on the notification opens the screen to download.Â 

  1. Unzip the zip file by selecting "Extract All" and open the questiondb.xml file withÂ a text or code editor (Notepad is sufficient).Â 

Â When you select "Extract All" a window will pop up showing you the new folder that is being created. If you are happy with the destination, select "Extract".

  1. Find the new folder that you just created during the Extract All process and open it.

Inside of this folder you will see three items: imsmanifest.xml, questiondb.xml, and a config folder. You will locate the questiondb.xml file.

  1. Â Open the questiondb.xml file in Notepad by right clicking on the file name and selecting "Open with -->Notepad"
  1. Â In Notepad, click Ctrl + H to open Find/Replace. Find all instances of render_choice shuffle="no" and replace them with render_choice shuffle="yes"

  1. Save the questiondb.xml file by simply selecting "Save" (this will overwrite the original file).
  2. Go back to the folder you created during "Extract All" and select all three files/folder that you see. You will now compress the folder back into a zip fileÂ 
    1. On Windows, right-click and choose Compress to ... --> ZIP File
    2. On a Mac, right-click on the folder and choose CompressÂ 

Â 

  1. A new, renamable zip file will be created. Rename this zip file so you will be able to identify it during the import process.
  1. Â Return to your Question Library in D2L and click Import. Choose Upload a File and upload the new zip file that you just created and renamed.Â 
  1. Â After checking the new questions to ensure that answer order shuffle has been enabled, delete the original questions from the Question LibraryÂ (note - the old question set and the new, shuffled question set will have the same name).

Â 

Â 

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

Â 5/01/2025

Â Kristen Dunlap

articles.title: Appraisal of SME/PL Review Comments Process
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to appraise SME PL review comments of a redesigned or newly designed course. The appraiser determines if a comment qualifies for a revision in the course templateâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to appraise SME PL review comments of a redesigned or newly designed course. The appraiser determines if a comment qualifies for a revision in the course templateâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to appraise SME PL review comments of a redesigned or newly designed course. The appraiser determines if a comment qualifies for a revision in the course template, should be saved for the second iteration of the course, or are out-of-scope and can be disregarded.

PROCEDUREÂ 

The SME PL review comments will be appraised by at least one of these people: QA team manager, ID Project lead, and ID team manager.

1. Locate the Build Changes and Post Build Review document for the redesigned or newly designed course. It will be saved in the Final Ready for Build and QA Review folder.

2. Locate the SME/PL Review table of each module (It has pink cells and is below the larger QA Phase Review table.)

3. Using the build map as a guide, determine the type of issue.

Types of Issues

Errors

Clear errors are either mistakes made during design (like indicating a wrong answer) or mistakes make during build (like adding the wrong question).

How to address errors

Note in the grey column that the error needs to be addressed by either the ITS team or the MM team. Change the pink fill to yellow. Instruct ITS and MM to change the yellow fill to green once the issue is been addressed.

After reviewing the remaining tables, alert ITS and MM in the group chat that there are issues in the SME/PL Review tables that need to be addressed.
Suggestions That Are Out of Scope

Any design suggestions that should have been determined during the design phase*, such as adding more content, and changes to boilerplate language** are out-of-scope.

How to address errors

Note in the grey column that the suggestion is out-of-scope, but that the suggestions will be saved for consideration in future designs. Change the pink fill to green to indicate that the SME/PL note has been addressed.

*Legitimate suggestions that apply to that specific course can be saved to a new document (titled SME PL Feedback for Future CourseXXXX Iterations) that is saved to the course folder (outside any course project folder.

Legitimate suggestions that could apply to multiple sections or all ONL courses are saved to a new document (titled SME PL Feedback for All Courses) that is saved in Quality Assurance channel.

**QA team collects boilerplate language feedback and saves the Quality Assurance channel.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

4.15.25

A Lakin, T Butts, C Cuatt, L Riggs

NA

articles.title: CF-Outlook Calendar PTO or OOO Documentation Process-240813
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document your scheduled PTO or time out of office (OOO) in your Outlook calendar so that others on the team will see you are not available if they need to scheâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document your scheduled PTO or time out of office (OOO) in your Outlook calendar so that others on the team will see you are not available if they need to scheâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document your scheduled PTO or time out of office (OOO) in your Outlook calendar so that others on the team will see you are not available if they need to schedule a meeting.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Part 1, Set Your Calendar to Out of Office

  1. Open Outlook and open the Outlook Calendar .
  2. Select New Appointment.
  1. Enter the details of your PTO or OOO time.
    1. A title for your own reference
    2. The date(s) and start and end times
    3. Show as Out of Office
    4. Reminder, if needed
  1. Select Save & Close.

Part 2, Alert Colleagues of Your PTO or OOO Status

  1. Start the process once again, this time selecting New Meeting.
  1. Enter a title that includes your name and "PTO" or "OOO." For example: Amy Lakin on PTO or Amy Lakin OOO, on-site in Tampa
  2. Add the individuals who you'd like to alert to your absence, including your manager/supervisor and the Curriculum project managers, in the Required area.
  3. Select the date(s) and start and end times.
  4. Show as Free, and do not set a Reminder.
  1. Select Send.
It's important that you set the status as "Free" so that your colleagues do not appear to others as OOO.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

8.13.24

A Lakin

articles.title: QA_Pre-Textbook Update Course Review Process_8.30.24
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this procedure is to conduct a "deep dive" quality review of a course that is scheduled for a textbook update (using the Curriculum Team Course Design Rubric). PROCEDUREÂ . 1.â¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this procedure is to conduct a "deep dive" quality review of a course that is scheduled for a textbook update (using the Curriculum Team Course Design Rubric). PROCEDUREÂ . 1.â¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this procedure is to conduct a "deep dive" quality review of a course that is scheduled for a textbook update (using the Curriculum Team Course Design Rubric).

PROCEDUREÂ 

1. Locate the Curriculum Team Course Design Rubric.

2. Open the document in the Desktop and save as Course #_Curriculum Team Course Design Rubric (insert the course number where indicated in the title).

3. Confirm with ITS the most recent D2L course template.

The QA Senior Manager will review end of course survey data and grades/assignment completions in PowerBI and will note any potential areas of concern in the sections titled Review Prior to Evaluation: EOC Surveys and Instructor Video Analysis and Review Prior to Evaluation: Assignment Submission Rates & Grades.

4. Enroll yourself as a student in the course.

5a. Evaluate the course using the rubric and complete the section titled Evaluation: UMA Course Design Rubric.

5b. Complete all assignments and activities as a student. Note any usability issues (such as broken links, functionality errors) in the section titled Usability Review.

6. The Senior QA Manager will analyze the evaluation and compile recommendations for next steps in the section titled Summary of Results and Suggested Next Steps.

7. Recommendations will be communicated to the Instructional Design team during the pre-planning phase of the textbook update project.

Amy and Craig need to specify how this communication will occur.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

8.30.24

A Lakin

articles.title: ID-Design to Build Handoff process
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document the process of retrieving SME-created extra credit and reviewing it for alignment. PROCEDUREÂ . Provide SME with the content map. Retrieve the completeâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to document the process of retrieving SME-created extra credit and reviewing it for alignment. PROCEDUREÂ . Provide SME with the content map. Retrieve the completeâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to outline the steps IDs will perform to transition a Course development project from the Formulation workflows, including Planning and Design Stages, to the Production workflows that include the Create, Build, and QA stages.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Once the Product Owner (PO) has provided approval for all content maps, IDs will begin the Deign to Build handoff by performing the following tasks:

  1. Perform Internal review (IR) on each map to ensure comments and tracked changes are removed, non-build team notes are also removed, and any errors are corrected so that the Maps are "Build ready". Save the files separately in the Final for Build folder.
  2. Optional: Lock all build maps to prevent editing, while allowing the functionality for the Production teams to make comments on which items they have questions.
  3. Make sure the Production team Managers and Leads are added to the project group chat.
  4. Distribute the link to the Final for Build folder by sharing the link in the project group chat and creating a message on the project board with the link to the production team Managers and Leads for review.
  5. Monitor communications for notifications of comments in the build maps and collaborate with the respective build team member(s) to overcome any challenges while minimizing any and all changes to the approved design
    ***Note: Build Maps are "Finished" designs, and no edits should be required at this point in the project that could require reapproval of an adjusted design. If there is concern that the need for change may require reapproval, escalate the issue to the Project Lead and Design Manager for review and a decision on next steps.
  6. Note any agreed upon changes, no matter how small in the appropriate documentation space for QA team awareness.
  7. TBD: Collect itemized count of assets each team must deliver? Confirm Production Teams have provided CPMO with deliverables and timeline? Collect Production Team signatures?
  8. Attend Build Kick-off meeting to answer questions regarding design.
  9. Mark the Design to Build Handoff complete on the project board.

RESOURCES

Not applicable

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

11/05/2024

Â Craig Cuatt

Â

Purpose

The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to delete questions from quizzes.

Procedure

  1. Go to Course Admin
  2. Under Assessments click Quizzes
  3. Select the quiz you want to edit
  4. Find and check the question(s) you want to delete
  5. Under More Actions, click Delete
  6. Click Save and Close

articles.title: ITS - Project Instructions - Updating Student Help Section - Classic
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to replace the Student Help module (classic) with the updated version (March 2025). PROCEDUREÂ . 1. Go to the template. 1a. Go to Start Here. 1b. Go to Getting Staâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to replace the Student Help module (classic) with the updated version (March 2025). PROCEDUREÂ . 1. Go to the template. 1a. Go to Start Here. 1b. Go to Getting Staâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to replace the Student Help module (classic) with the updated version (March 2025).

PROCEDUREÂ 

1. Go to the template.

1a. Go to Start Here.

1b. Go to Getting Started - Student Help module.

2. Change the name of the Student Help Module to "OLD Student Help"

3. Go back to the Getting Started module.

4. Select the Upload / Create button and select âAdd Object from LORâ.

6. Search for âStudent Help Section - Classic Template Update 2025â. Press the Search button.

7. Scroll down to select âStudent Help Section - Classic Template Update 2025â.

Click the circle to the left to select it and the Next button.

9. Select âDynamic Link to Newest Versionâ and the Next button.

10. Select the Create Topic button.

11. The new Student Help module called Student Help Section - Classic Template Update 2025 will be added.

(Move it up to above the Instructor Cengage activation links in Getting Started.)

12. Click into the "Add a description..." area of the "OLD Student Help" and Ctrl-A and Ctrl-C to copy all.

13. Click into the "Add a description..." area of the new Student Help. Ctrl-V to paste all.

14. Then, for each link, go to Edit Properties in Place.

15. Remove "10-22-2024" from each of the 7 links. (Do NOT click to open in a new window.)

16. Make each link Not Required.

17. Click to the module and change name to "Student Help".

18. LAST STEP -- Delete the "OLD Student Help" module.

----------

For QA:

In the updated Student Help:

  • The Student Support Teams & Technical Self-Help link is now called Technical Resources
  • New banners
  • The Discussion Help page has fewer links.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

4.25.25

L Walent

articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to show the steps to create badges in D2L Brightspace LMS.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. Â Coordinate badge file creation with the Multimedia team.
  2. Upload badges to Manage Files. (This is just a backup as they will be imported directly to badges area.)
  3. Go to Course Admin - Awards - Course Awards tab - and then click on Add Award to Course button.
  1. Click on Create button.
  2. Add badge name, description and criteria.
  3. Â  Choose Award Type: Badge, Make this Award Available to all of my Courses, and Expiry: Never.
  4. Choose Award Image - Click on the Upload New Icon button.
  5. Upload new icon image from your desktop or downloads folder. Be sure that the file is not too large or it will not upload.
  6. Do not change the settings here and make sure Use this award in [your course] now is checked.
  7. Click on the Save and Close button.
  8. Go to Badges and you'll see your newly created badge there. Than, click on the Edit Properties button.
  9. We normally don't add credits or hide the award. If the Content Map calls for it, add it here.
  10. To create conditions, click on the Create button.
  11. Then you can click on the Select Condition Type button.
  12. Then you can Create a Release ConditionUnder -- Select Condition Type -- choose the condition, usually a visited item or a graded item.
  13. p
  14. Or, for example, you can select a Release Condition of Score on an assessment.
  15. Then you can select Condition Details of Complete: Discussion Quiz.

articles.title: Cengage - Troubleshooting Browser Issues in Cengage
articles.description: The purpose of this SOP is to troubleshoot browser issues within Cengage. First, make sure your browser and operating system are fully up to date. You would also need to do a cache/cookie clear (alsoâ¦
articles.short_version: The purpose of this SOP is to troubleshoot browser issues within Cengage. First, make sure your browser and operating system are fully up to date. You would also need to do a cache/cookie clear (alsoâ¦
articles.body:
The purpose of this SOP is to troubleshoot browser issues within Cengage.

  1. First, make sure your browser and operating system are fully up to date. You would also need to do a cache/cookie clear (also sometimes referred to as browser history clear). See the instructions below for the appropriate browser used on your device:

Â 

http://www.whatismybrowser.com

Â 

  • Browser troubleshooting Steps in Safari:

Â 

  1. Click System Preferences (system settings) in the upper-left corner of the browser window.
  2. Then select "Privacy"
  3. Disable or turn off "Prevent cross-site tracking".
  4. Uncheck the "Block all cookies" field.
  5. Click "Manage Website Data" and select "Remove All" (this will log you out of website login sessions).
  6. On the "Websites" tab, turn "Content Blockers" off, or disable them.
  7. While also on "Websites" tab, scroll to "Pop-up Windows" and change to allow pop-up windows.

Â 

  • MS Edge browser troubleshooting steps:

Â 

  1. Click the three horizontal dots in the upper-right corner of the browser window, underneath the (X) symbol to close the window.
  2. Select "Settings"
  3. On the left side navigation column, select "Privacy, search, and services".
  4. Under "Clear browsing data", click "Choose what to clear"
  5. Change the time range to "All time"
  6. Check only the boxes for "Cookies and other site data" and "Cached images and files"
  7. Click "Clear now"
  8. On the left side navigation column, select "Cookies and site permissions"Â 
  9. Scroll to select "Pop-ups and redirects"
  10. Make sure the "Blocked (recommended) slider option is turned off (left)

Â 

  • Browser troubleshooting Steps in Google Chrome:

Â 

  1. Click the three vertical dots in the upper-right corner of the browser window, underneath the (X) symbol to close the window.
  2. Select "Settings"
  3. On the left side navigation column, select "Privacy and Security".
  4. Select Site Settings
  5. Scroll down to "Content" and select "Pop-ups and Redirects"
  6. Change the setting to "Sites can send pop-ups and use redirects"
  7. Return to previous page by clicking the back arrow at the top of the column
  8. Select "Cookies and site data" from the "Content" section
  9. Select "Allow all/third-party cookies"
  10. On the left side navigation column, select "Privacy and Security".
  11. Click on "Clear Browsing data", it should be the first option under Privacy and Security in the middle of your screen here.
  12. Change the time range to "All Time".
  13. Check the box next to "Cookies and other site data" as well as the box next to "Cached images and files".
  14. Click "Clear Data".

Â 

  • If browser issues persist, students should call:

Phone: 800-354-9706

Sunday: 6PM-12AM ET

Monday-Thursday: 8AM-12AM ET

Friday: 8AM-6PM ET

PURPOSE

The purpose of this SOP is to provide a workaround for students having issues getting grades to submit for Cengage Bongo video assignments, for example in SO1100 Interactive 2A and 2C.

PROCEDURE

1. Goto Chrome

2) Goto the url and type chrome://flags/

Â 

3)Â In search flags type in hardware-accelerated video

4) Goto the chevron for Hardware-accelerated video decode

5) Change to Hardware-accelerated video decode to Disabled

6) Close Chrome and then relaunch

7) Return to the interactive, you should now see a Submit For Grading button

  1. Click content

  1. Click Course Admin

  3. Click the drop-down menu and click edit
  4. Under Availability Dates and Conditions change the date to your desired start date
  5. Click save and close

articles.title: ITS-Creating Surveys Using the D2L Survey
articles.description: Purpose. The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to create a survey in D2L. Procedure. Survey Set-up. Go to Course Admin. Go to the Assessments section and click Survey. Click New Survey. Fill in tâ¦
articles.short_version: Purpose. The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to create a survey in D2L. Procedure. Survey Set-up. Go to Course Admin. Go to the Assessments section and click Survey. Click New Survey. Fill in tâ¦
articles.body:
Purpose

The purpose of this SOP is to explain how to create a survey in D2L.

Procedure

Survey Set-up

  1. Go to Course Admin
  2. Go to the Assessments section and click Survey
  3. Click New Survey
  4. Fill in the corresponding information
    1. Label survey Course Name Unit # Survey Questions
    2. Choose the corresponding category
    3. Check box to give instant feedback
    4. Do not check the box to make the survey anonymous
  5. To add questions, click Add/Edit Questions
    1. If you would like to create questions in the survey, click New. Then choose the type of question. The question types are: True/False, Multiple Choice, Multi-Select, Written Response, Short Answer, Multi-Short Answer, Fill in the Blank, Matching, Ordering, and Likert.
    2. If you would like to import questions, click Import, then choose Browse Question Library
  6. Finaly, go to the restrictions tab. Uncheck the box labeled Hide from users and enter the date the survey will become available.
  7. Click save.

How to add the survey into the course

  1. Go to the content tab.
  2. Select the unit you would like to add the survey to, then click Add existing.
  3. Click More, then click Surveys. Select the survey you would like to add.

articles.title: OA2500 - NHA Mock Exam Grades
articles.description: Accessing Grades in NHA. Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform. Log in. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center. Select Performanceâ¦
articles.short_version: Accessing Grades in NHA. Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform. Log in. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center. Select Performanceâ¦
articles.body:
Accessing Grades in NHA

Steps for logging in and accessing learner grades and progress in the NHA platform.

  1. Log in
  2. From the Instructor Dashboard, select Learning Insights Center
  1. Select Performance Analytics to see learner specific information for the course
  1. Choose the following content from the drop-down menu:
    1. Nasium Training, LLC
    2. Select course (scroll down until you see the live sections and look for the start date; hover your mouse cursor over the course to see the information appear)
    3. Select APPLY
  2. After the page refreshes, you'll see the course overview snapshot. A high level overview of all students will appear in the 3 boxes: In the past week; Module Progress, and Module Performance - Across Products
    1. Across Products = the different NHA content that has been placed in the course such as CCMA Study Guide, PersonAbility, etc.
    2. Select NEXT in that 3rd box (Module Performance - Across Products) for more detailed information

  1. Scroll down the page to find specific learners as well as the various tabs to view learner-specific work and completion data:

Finding Learner Grades

  1. Choose learner from the list; click on their blue hyperlink.
  2. Click on the ASSESSMENTS tab to access point values for each grade.
NOTE: Modules are not graded items. Clicking on the MODULES tab will show you whether a learner has accessed the content and completed the content.

  1. Scroll through the Assessments to find the point value.
    1. Below each score overview, you can find specific topics the learner may want to review before taking the next practice exam. Topics are recommended based on missed items on the exam.
  2. Back in the D2L gradebook, input the highest grade the learner received (found by scrolling through the learner info under the MODULES tab in the NHA platform).
    1. Be sure to add the points and NOT the percent.

  1. Do the same for the PersonAbility ESSENTIAL SKILLS and the PersonAbility FINAL ASSESSMENT grades.
    1. They are located in the same MODULES tab on the NHA platform where you found the CCMA Practice Exam scores.

articles.title: Respondus Pull from Nasium Server
articles.description: Respondus Pull from Nasium Server. Login with Nasium username and password (i.e., pdragon@nasiumtraining.com) Click on RETRIEVAL tab. Click Retrieve Questions.. Select NEXT for Nasium Prod.. Choose câ¦
articles.short_version: Respondus Pull from Nasium Server. Login with Nasium username and password (i.e., pdragon@nasiumtraining.com) Click on RETRIEVAL tab. Click Retrieve Questions.. Select NEXT for Nasium Prod.. Choose câ¦
articles.body:
Respondus Pull from Nasium Server

  1. Login with Nasium username and password (i.e., pdragon@nasiumtraining.com)
    1. Click on RETRIEVAL tab
    2. Click Retrieve Questions.
    3. Select NEXT for Nasium Prod.
    4. Choose course from the drop-down menu.
    5. Select Assessment Type of asset to pull.
      PRO TIP: Make sure assessment names are easily identified in the course for ease and efficiency in matching the content being pulled from Respondus. Update naming conventions in course prior to pulling from Respondus, if needed.
    6. Enter a name for the file; click NEXT.
      Note: If reuploading content to course after edits will be made, add something like "Respondus" to the end of the naming convention so it doesn't get mixed up with the new file that will be added to the course.
    7. Ensure retrieval process was successful; click FINISH.
    8. Click OK to save file
  2. Click on Preview + Publish tab
    1. Select Print Options
    2. If pulling something like a Self-Assessment or another assessment that includes feedback, select Exam with Answer Key
    1. Click on SETTINGS
      1. Select "Question Title" and/or "Feedback", if needed.
      2. Click OK.
    2. Click PRINT PREVIEW to view content, if wanted.
    3. Click SAVE TO FILE.
    1. Name the file on your computer and choose appropriate folder to save to.
    1. Save File
  1. Files ready to share with ID/SME.

articles.title: Embed Kaltura in a Web Page in D2L
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to illustrate how to embed a Kaltura Video into a Web Page. PROCEDUREÂ . 1. Multimedia Team may shares a link with you. Here is an example: https://mediaspace.ultiâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to illustrate how to embed a Kaltura Video into a Web Page. PROCEDUREÂ . 1. Multimedia Team may shares a link with you. Here is an example: https://mediaspace.ultiâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to illustrate how to embed a Kaltura Video into a Web Page.

PROCEDUREÂ 

1. Multimedia Team may shares a link with you. Here is an example:

https://mediaspace.ultimatemedical.edu/media/EN1150+Intro+2023/1_no19cnja

2. Otherwise follow these steps to get the video

Open your Webpage

2. Goto to Edit HTML

3. Go to Insert Stuff

4. Goto Embed Kaltura Media (Insert Stuff)

5. Goto Add from Library

6. Search for the video by name in My Media. Multimedia will often provide an identifier example: 1_uernqf3i that you can search for.

Note you have to type in the entire identifier to locate the video

7. One time only, Click on settings

8. Keep these settings or make sure this is your default

9.Click on Embed

10.You should see a preview with Annoto

11. Select Insert

12. Select Save

13. Goto code editor

14. This code needs to be changed to make room for Annoto

15. Replace this code starting with width

width="910">

16. With this NEW code

width="100%" style="border-style: none;">

17. Then hit Save

18. Make sure Annoto is present then select Save and Close

19. You should see the video load with Annoto seamlessly

articles.title: Add Mid Course Grade Week 1 - Week 3 Combined Grade Percentage
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to create a Mid Course Grade Week 1 - Week 3 Combined Grade Percentage and to hide C urrent Discussion Average (30 percentage of Total Grade), Current Assignmentâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to create a Mid Course Grade Week 1 - Week 3 Combined Grade Percentage and to hide C urrent Discussion Average (30 percentage of Total Grade), Current Assignmentâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to create a Mid Course Grade Week 1 - Week 3 Combined Grade Percentage and to hide Current Discussion Average (30 percentage of Total Grade), Current Assignment Average (25 percent of Total Grade), Current Assessment Average (45 percent of Total Grade) from students.

PROCEDUREÂ 

1)Â Â Go to the template

2) Go to Grades

3) Go to Manage Grades

4) Add New item

5) Choose Calculated

6) Enter the following text for both Name and Short Name:

Mid Course Grade Week 1 - Week 3 Combined Grade Percentage

7) Leave Category as None

8) Add the following text to Description

This is your current Mid-Course Grade percentage for Week 1, Week 2, and Week 3 combined.

9) Check the box next to Allow users to view grade item description

10) Check box for Can Exceed

11) Go to Restrictions

12) a. Select Has a Start Date and input this date and time (this way it opens up for students at the Week 3 Due Date)

2/4/2022 2:00 PM

b. Leave Has End Date unchecked. Students need to see this for the remainder of the semester

c. Click Save and Close

13) Scroll down to the bottom, you will see the clock icon indicating the column is date managed

14) Scroll to the top and select the drop down for More Actions

Then select Reorder

15) Change the Mid Course Grade to 1 then click Save

16) Scroll up and open the column again

a . Select all Week 1, Week 2, and Week 3

b. Then unselect Roll Call

17) Then click Save and Close

18) Last Step, Scroll down and hide Current Discussion Average (30 percentage of Total Grade), Current Assignment Average (25 percent of Total Grade), Current Assessment Average (45 percent of Total Grade) from students.

Students will see these changes

articles.title: Adding LOR Links Info Provided from D2L
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is go over the steps to use replacement strings when linking objects from the LOR. PROCEDUREÂ . When you copy content from one master course template to the cloned liâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is go over the steps to use replacement strings when linking objects from the LOR. PROCEDUREÂ . When you copy content from one master course template to the cloned liâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is go over the steps to use replacement strings when linking objects from the LOR.

PROCEDUREÂ 

When you copy content from one master course template to the cloned live courses, the URLs within the HTML topics are copied exactly as they are in the master course template.

  • If you use replace strings for the OrgUnitId in the master course template, the URL becomes relative and is copied with the replace strings as a relative URL to the cloned course. This is what happened with the example below where the code works.
  • If you use a fixed OrgUnitId in the master course template, the URL is absolute and is copied with the absolute OrgUnitId to the cloned course. This is what happened with the example below where the code did not work consistently for all users.

Â 

Here is an example. Suppose your master course template is ME1000_TEMPLATE_241021 with an OrgUnitId of 464685.

Â 

Replace Strings

Using replace strings for the OrgUnitId in the LOR image URL for OrgUnitId 464685:

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/{orgUnitId}/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

OrgUnitId in the LOR image URL after cloning for OrgUnitId 567890:

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/{orgUnitId}/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

Â 

With the above structure, it doesnât matter what new org unit is created when you clone the master course template; the replace strings will result in a relative URL being copied to the cloned course. So, any user with any role will be able to see the image.

Â 

Fixed OUID

Using a fixed OrgUnitId in the LOR image URL for OrgUnitId 464685:

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/464685/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

OrgUnitId in the LOR image URL after cloning for new OrgUnitId 567890:

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/464685/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

Â 

With the above structure, when new org units are created during cloning, they retain the fixed URL in the cloned course. The user experience in this case depends on whether the user is enrolled in the original master course template.

  • The image will display for all users enrolled with the Curriculum Team (C) role because the role has cascading access to ALL org units, including both the cloned course (OrgUnitId 567890) AND the template (OrgUnitId 464685).
  • Users enrolled in the cloned course (OrgUnitId 567890) with the Instructor or Student role will NOT see the image unless they are ALSO enrolled in the template (OrgUnitId 464685).

Â 

In your second example below, youâll notice that:

  • The OrgUnitId of the cloned course offering is 386641.
  • The OrgUnitId referenced in the URL of the first image is 102970.
  • The OrgUnitId referenced in the URL of the second and third images is 102914.
  • Therefore, to see the all the images in the OUID 386641 course offering, the user would also have to be enrolled in both 102970 and 102914.

Â 

In short, if you plan on using the LOR to house images going forward, you must ensure that all master course templates have image URLs which use replace strings for the OrgUnitId. These will then be copied with replace strings to the cloned courses, making the images visible to all users.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is go over the steps to use replacement strings when linking objects from the LOR.

PROCEDUREÂ 

When you copy content from one master course template to the cloned live courses, the URLs within the HTML topics are copied exactly as they are in the master course template.

  • If you use replace strings for the OrgUnitId in the master course template, the URL becomes relative and is copied with the replace strings as a relative URL to the cloned course. This is what happened with the example below where the code works.
  • If you use a fixed OrgUnitId in the master course template, the URL is absolute and is copied with the absolute OrgUnitId to the cloned course. This is what happened with the example below where the code did not work consistently for all users.

Â 

Here is an example. Suppose your master course template is ME1000_TEMPLATE_241021 with an OrgUnitId of 464685.

Using replace strings for the OrgUnitId in the LOR image URL for OrgUnitId 464685:

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/{orgUnitId}/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

<img src="/d2l/lor/viewer/viewFile.d2lfile/464685/4090,-1/" alt="Select Instructor Messaging in Discussion" title="Instructor Messaging Communications 1" style="max-width: 100%; display: block; margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;">

Â 

In your second example below, youâll notice that:

  • The OrgUnitId of the cloned course offering is 386641.

articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to provide instructions for using BrowserStack Live Cross-Browser testing on real devices.

PROCEDUREÂ 

I. Overview and Getting Started

1)Â Â Â  Go to the login for BrowserStack

https://www.browserstack.com/users/sign_in

Use your UMA email to login, but delete any password

Â 

You will then see that SSO is enabled, select Login

2)Â Â Â  Hover over the chevron next to Products.

3)Â Â Â  Select Live Cross Browser Testing

Â 

4)Â Â Â  You can choose from iOS (Apple), Android, Windows Desktop, or Mac.

5)Â Â Â  Here is an example of getting started with a real device by opening an iPhone.

a.Â Â Â Â  Hovering the mouse point over iPhone 14, you are given the option to select Safari or Chrome.

Â 

b.Â Â Â  After selecting Safari, it will take a few seconds for the device to load.

c.Â Â Â Â  Shortly after the last step for Optimizing for performance loads,

Â 

The real device will load for you to begin testing. The image below shows the device and a draggable tool bar.

Â 

Â 

II.Â Using the Tool bar

Â 

First we will go over the different testing options located on the tool bar. Some of these options are available but not needed. In a single tab multi-device testing session, the Live toolbar gets divided into two categories: Single Device Controls and All Device Controls.

Â 

1.Â Â Â  Single Device Testing Tool bar

Â 

Below is a screen shot of the single device testing toolbar. We will go over each option.

a.Â Â Â Â  Switch Browser

Selecting Switch browser will typically require you to end your existing session, resulting in this screen asking you to continue the existing session (ex. Safari for an iPhone 14)or picking a new browserÂ 

You can choose a different browser by hovering over the device, for this example the browser will be changed to Chrome by hovering over the device browser and selecting Chrome

Â 

This results in a new session, notice in the screen shot below the iPhone 14 loading is using Chrome as a browser.

Â 

If you are unable to get this to load, try clicking on Stop Session, refreshing your the browser you are using BrowserStack in, and starting a new session with the desired browser.

b.Â Â Â  Local Testing

(DO NOT USE)

Local testing requires UMA to install the desktop app on your laptop, we will not be using Local Testing.

Â 

Â 

Â 

c.Â Â Â Â  Zoom

Â 

Zoom allows you to increase or decrease the size of the device on your screen.

Â 

Clicking on the â sign makes the device smaller

Â 

Â 

Clicking on the + sign makes the device bigger

Â 

Â 

Â d.Â Â Â  Capture a bug

This settings allows you to point out issues with what you are seeing and capture an image

Â 

The mark up options are as follows:

Â 

Once you have completed your screen shot you can download it by selecting Download Image

Â 

Â 

You can then open the image in your downloads folder

Â 

You can then open or share the image

Â 

Â 

After you have saved your image, click on "Cancel"

Â 

You will be asked if you want to Discard your screen shot, now that you have downloaded it,Â Â 

Click on Discard

Â 

You will then return to your session

Â 

e.Â Â Â  Record Session

Â 

This settings allows you to record a video of what you are seeing

Â 

Select Entire Screen and choose which screen you would like to record, then click on Share.

Â 

You session will start recording,

Â 

Once you are finished click on Stop Sharing.

Â 

SelectÂ  Download to save your session.

Your session will show up in your downloads folder.

Â 

Â 

f.Â Â Â Â Â  DevTools

DevTools are available for Chrome and Safari only.

Chrome provides the following:

Â 

Â Elements

Â 

Â 

Console

Â Sources

Â 

Network

Application

Â 

Security

Safari provides:

Elements, Console, Sources, Network,

Â 

Timelines

Â 

Storage

Â 

Graphics

Layers

Audits

Â 

For Android devices, Dev Tools can be accessed only after selecting Chrome.

Â 

Â 

Other browsers result in DevTools being grayed outÂ 

Â 

g.Â Â Â Â  Settings

Settings includes Self Signed Certificate Support, Idle session timeout, Dark mode, General and for this example, Safari Browser settings.

Â Â 

  • Self Signed Certificate Support

Â 

This should stay on Disable, we will not be using it. (if IT for more info see this link Testing Secure Sites Using Self-Signed Certificates | BrowserStack Docs)

Â 

Â 

Â 

Â 

Â 

  • Idle session timeout

Â Change the default time for your sessions to time out or current session.

  • Dark Mode

You can change this Display and Brightness setting.

  • General

You can Set Time and Date.

Â 

  • Accessibility

Change accessibility settings such as contrast, text size, and reduce motion.

Â 

  • Safari

Change Safari settings

You can adjust Prevent Cross-Site Tracking and Block Pop-ups

Â 

Â 

h.Â Â Â  Show Home Screen

Takes you to the device home screen

i.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Rotate Device

This setting allows you to rotate the device. This is a useful setting as some functionality issues have occurred in D2L after rotating a device.

Â 

This is the default setting

After rotating

j.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Change Location

Use this setting to change the geographic location of the device

Â 

k.Â Â Â Â  Files & Media

Use this setting to upload files from the device

And download files from the device

Image Injection is not supported on all devices, for more information click on the link below:

Â 

https://www.browserstack.com/docs/live/media/image-injection#Device-_browser_combinations

Â 

l.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Throttle Network

This setting allows you to set the download and upload speed, packet loss, and latency. For more information use this link: Testing Secure Sites Using Self-Signed Certificates | BrowserStack Docs

Â 

Â 

Â 

You can select Offline.

Â 

You can also create a custom profile to create your own network profile. Then, click Apply.

Â 

Â 

For more information regarding latency here is a link Understanding latency - Web performance | MDN (mozilla.org)

Â 

m. Screen Reader

This setting is not available for all devices.

Â 

Here is an example using an Android device. After you toggle the button to turn on Screen Reader,

Make sure you have your speakers enabled so you can hear the voice over!

Â 

Â 

n.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Device info can be found here:

Â 

Â 

o.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Stop Session

Use this button to end your current session

Â 

Â 2.Â Â Â  Multi Device Testing Tool bar

The tool bar using two devices for parallel testing has less features. Parallel device testing will allow you to view the course on two different devices and if needs two different browsers.

a. Select your first preferred device-browser combination to run your website or web app.

You will see that only the single device toolbar is present

b. Select Add second device

c. Select your second preferred device-browser combination.

d. You will see both devices on your screen, select Got it

e. Select View Settings

f. Toggle the Sync interactions across devices to on

Some devices do not have the option to Sync, you will get a notification. Here is an example:

Then click on the chevron to close the pop out

g. Once logged into the same course in D2L, navigating through the course on one device will automatically occur on the other device

h. The All Device Controls toolbar has less features than single device

i. The All Device Control toolbar has less features than single device. Select Rotate Device

both devices will then rotate

j. DevTools are available for Browsers

k. To end your parallel device session, click on Stop All Devices.

Use this link to view a list of browsers & mobile devices for live cross browser testing

https://www.browserstack.com/list-of-browsers-and-platforms/live

III. iOS Apple

Â 

Choose from a selection of iPhones, Be sure to check if other devices are available under Show More Devices.

You can also choose devices With SIM.

Choose devices with SIM only used in the USA.

Â 

And choose SIM devices specifically available with T-Mobile as a carrier.

Â 

Choose from a list of iPads

IV. Android

Samsung phones and tablets

articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to provide instructions for using BrowserStack Live Cross-Browser testing on real devices.

A video of a live training session is available here.

PROCEDUREÂ 

I. Overview and Getting Started

1) Let's get started!Â Â Â 

a. Go to MyApps Desktop app

b. Click on BrowserStack Single Sign OnÂ 

Â 

c. Wait for it to load which make take a minute

Â d. You should see this screen

Â 

Â 

2)Â Â Â  Hover over the chevron next to Products.

3)Â Â Â  Select Live Cross Browser Testing

Â 

4)Â Â Â  You can choose from iOS (Apple), Android, Windows Desktop, or Mac.

5)Â Â Â  Here is an example of getting started with a real device by opening an iPhone.

a.Â Â Â Â  Hovering the mouse point over iPhone 14, you are given the option to select Safari or Chrome.

Â 

b.Â Â Â  After selecting Safari, it will take a few seconds for the device to load.

c.Â Â Â Â  Shortly after the last step for Optimizing for performance loads,

Â 

The real device will load for you to begin testing. The image below shows the device and a draggable tool bar.

Â 

Â 

II.Â Using the Tool bar

Â 

First we will go over the different testing options located on the tool bar. Some of these options are available but not needed. In a single tab multi-device testing session, the Live toolbar gets divided into two categories: Single Device Controls and All Device Controls.

Â 

1.Â Â Â  Single Device Testing Tool bar

Â 

Below is a screen shot of the single device testing toolbar. We will go over each option.

a.Â Â Â Â  Switch Browser

Selecting Switch browser will typically require you to end your existing session, resulting in this screen asking you to continue the existing session (ex. Safari for an iPhone 14)or picking a new browserÂ 

You can choose a different browser by hovering over the device, for this example the browser will be changed to Chrome by hovering over the device browser and selecting Chrome

Â 

This results in a new session, notice in the screen shot below the iPhone 14 loading is using Chrome as a browser.

Â 

If you are unable to get this to load, try clicking on Stop Session, refreshing your the browser you are using BrowserStack in, and starting a new session with the desired browser.

b.Â Â Â  Local Testing

(DO NOT USE)

Local testing requires UMA to install the desktop app on your laptop, we will not be using Local Testing.

Â 

Â 

Â 

c.Â Â Â Â  Zoom

Â 

Zoom allows you to increase or decrease the size of the device on your screen.

Â 

Clicking on the â sign makes the device smaller

Â 

Â 

Clicking on the + sign makes the device bigger

Â 

Â 

Â d.Â Â Â  Capture a bug

This settings allows you to point out issues with what you are seeing and capture an image

Â 

The mark up options are as follows:

Â 

Once you have completed your screen shot you can download it by selecting Download Image

Â 

Â 

You can then open the image in your downloads folder

Â 

You can then open or share the image

Â 

Â 

After you have saved your image, click on "Cancel"

Â 

You will be asked if you want to Discard your screen shot, now that you have downloaded it,Â Â 

Click on Discard

Â 

You will then return to your session

Â 

e.Â Â Â  Record Session

Â 

This settings allows you to record a video of what you are seeing

Â 

Select Entire Screen and choose which screen you would like to record, then click on Share.

Â 

You session will start recording,

Â 

Once you are finished click on Stop Sharing.

Â 

SelectÂ  Download to save your session.

Your session will show up in your downloads folder.

Â 

Â 

f.Â Â Â Â Â  DevTools

DevTools are available for Chrome and Safari only.

Chrome provides the following:

Â 

Â Elements

Â 

Â 

Console

Â Sources

Â 

Network

Application

Â 

Security

Safari provides:

Elements, Console, Sources, Network,

Â 

Timelines

Â 

Storage

Â 

Graphics

Layers

Audits

Â 

For Android devices, Dev Tools can be accessed only after selecting Chrome.

Â 

Â 

Other browsers result in DevTools being grayed outÂ 

Â 

g.Â Â Â Â  Settings

Settings includes Self Signed Certificate Support, Idle session timeout, Dark mode, General and for this example, Safari Browser settings.

Â Â 

  • Self Signed Certificate Support

Â 

This should stay on Disable, we will not be using it. (if IT for more info see this link Testing Secure Sites Using Self-Signed Certificates | BrowserStack Docs)

Â 

Â 

Â 

Â 

Â 

  • Idle session timeout

Â Change the default time for your sessions to time out or current session.

  • Dark Mode

You can change this Display and Brightness setting.

  • General

You can Set Time and Date.

Â 

  • Accessibility

Change accessibility settings such as contrast, text size, and reduce motion.

Â 

  • Safari

Change Safari settings

You can adjust Prevent Cross-Site Tracking and Block Pop-ups

Â 

Â 

h.Â Â Â  Show Home Screen

Takes you to the device home screen

i.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Rotate Device

This setting allows you to rotate the device. This is a useful setting as some functionality issues have occurred in D2L after rotating a device.

Â 

This is the default setting

After rotating

j.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Change Location

Use this setting to change the geographic location of the device

Â 

k.Â Â Â Â  Files & Media

Use this setting to upload files from the device

And download files from the device

Image Injection is not supported on all devices, for more information click on the link below:

Â 

https://www.browserstack.com/docs/live/media/image-injection#Device-_browser_combinations

Â 

l.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Throttle Network

This setting allows you to set the download and upload speed, packet loss, and latency. For more information use this link: Testing Secure Sites Using Self-Signed Certificates | BrowserStack Docs

Â 

Â 

Â 

You can select Offline.

Â 

You can also create a custom profile to create your own network profile. Then, click Apply.

Â 

Â 

For more information regarding latency here is a link Understanding latency - Web performance | MDN (mozilla.org)

Â 

m. Screen Reader

This setting is not available for all devices.

Â 

Here is an example using an Android device. After you toggle the button to turn on Screen Reader,

Make sure you have your speakers enabled so you can hear the voice over!

Â 

Â 

n.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Device info can be found here:

Â 

Â 

o.Â Â Â Â Â Â  Stop Session

Use this button to end your current session

Â 

Â 2.Â Â Â  Multi Device Testing Tool bar

The tool bar using two devices for parallel testing has less features. Parallel device testing will allow you to view the course on two different devices and if needs two different browsers.

a. Select your first preferred device-browser combination to run your website or web app.

You will see that only the single device toolbar is present

b. Select Add second device

c. Select your second preferred device-browser combination.

d. You will see both devices on your screen, select Got it

e. Select View Settings

f. Toggle the Sync interactions across devices to on

Some devices do not have the option to Sync, you will get a notification. Here is an example:

Then click on the chevron to close the pop out

g. Once logged into the same course in D2L, navigating through the course on one device will automatically occur on the other device

h. The All Device Controls toolbar has less features than single device

i. The All Device Control toolbar has less features than single device. Select Rotate Device

both devices will then rotate

j. DevTools are available for Browsers

k. To end your parallel device session, click on Stop All Devices.

Use this link to view a list of browsers & mobile devices for live cross browser testing

https://www.browserstack.com/list-of-browsers-and-platforms/live

III. iOS Apple

Â 

Choose from a selection of iPhones, Be sure to check if other devices are available under Show More Devices.

You can also choose devices With SIM.

Choose devices with SIM only used in the USA.

Â 

And choose SIM devices specifically available with T-Mobile as a carrier.

Â 

Choose from a list of iPads

IV. Android

Samsung phones and tablets

Â Google phones

OnePlus phones

Motorola phones

Xiaomi smart phones

Vivo phones

Oppo phones

Huawei phones

V. Windows

Windows 11

Other versions

VI. Mac

articles.title: QA_Clearwater Copyedit and QA Course Review Process_250506
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to review newly designed and redesigned courses for the Clearwater (CL) ground campus in the role of a copyeditor and QA specialist. A separate process is used toâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this SOP is to review newly designed and redesigned courses for the Clearwater (CL) ground campus in the role of a copyeditor and QA specialist. A separate process is used toâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to review newly designed and redesigned courses for the Clearwater (CL) ground campus in the role of a copyeditor and QA specialist. A separate process is used to review the course in the student role.

SPECIAL NOTE

We only edit mechanical errors (punctuation, grammar, or spelling) and briefly revise sentences for clarity, conciseness, and to maintain parallelism. Any more detailed revisions, such as rearranging the sentence order or adding phrases or sentences, should be documented in the QA Review document. If you feel major revisions are necessary, provide your suggestions in the QA Review document only.

Refer to the Editing Text Within a D2L Course job aid for editing instructions.

=

PROCEDUREÂ 

I. Prepare for Review

  1. Request the CL director (currently Ann Jackson) to add you to the course as an instructor and as a student.
  2. Create a new copy of the CL Template QA Review document:
  • Go to the Quality Assurance channel in the EDU Curricular Affairs team.
  • Select the CL Course Reviews and CE folder.

=

  • Select the document CL Template QA Review; it will open in the browser.
  • Select Create a Copy from the File menu, then Create a copy online.

=

  • In the pop-up window, change the word "Template" in the File Name to the course number and delete "- Copy."

=

  1. Add the course number in the header area of the document.
  2. Add your name as reviewer and the date(s) of review.

=

=

II. Access the Course in D2L

  1. Log into D2L using your standard login and password.
  2. Click on the Select a course icon (box with 9 smaller boxes) and enter the course number. Clearwater courses typically have "CL" at the end of the number.
  3. Select the course link.

=

  1. Once you access the course, dismiss the welcome pop-up window.
  2. Select Coursework from the nav bar at the top of the page.

==

=

III. Review and Edit Course Materials: Instructor - Course Information Module

  1. Skip the overview text of the Instructor - Course Information module because it is boilerplate language that has been edited previously.

=

  1. Skip the overview text of the Instructor Guides for In-Class Activities submodule because it is also boilerplate language that has been edited previously.
  • Items within the Instructor Guides for In-Class Activities submodule: Ensure that the individual Instructor Guides match the course title (meaning, the correct guides are in the course). They do not need to be copyedited. If the Instructor Guides are missing or are for a different course, note the issue in the QA Review document.

=

  1. Skip the overview text of the Handouts/Printed Materials for Application submodule.
  • Items within the Handouts/Printed Materials for Application submodule: Review these documents which likely cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.

=

  1. Skip the Publisher Resources module as it contains items supplied by the publisher that we cannot edit.

=

  1. Skip the Lab Comps submodule overview text.
  • Review the documents within the Lab Comps submodule. These are likely documents that cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.
Typically, the Clearwater director does not like to change the language of lab comps as it is standard language that is supplied by the state. However, note any glaring errors like misspellings or missing text.

=

  1. Skip the LTI Links - Cengage submodule.
  2. Skip Instructor Resources Course.
  3. Skip Instructor's Guide to Ticket Submission and Teams Posts.
  4. Skip Checklist (For Developers Only).

==

=

IV. Review and Edit Course Materials: Student-Facing Modules

Welcome Module

  1. Review the unique text in the Welcome module overview:
  • The first line should indicate the correct course number.
  • The course description should have been added as the first full paragraph.
  • Compare the text to the course name and description in the syllabus.
  1. Skip the boilerplate language beginning with "Your participation in this course..."
  2. Skip the items within the Welcome module as they all use boilerplate language.

=

=

Getting Started - Important Information Module

  1. Skip the overview text in the Getting Started module.

=

  1. Skip the overview text in the Course Design and Schedule submodule.

=

  1. Review and edit if necessary the unique text in the Syllabus, which includes
  • the course name and number
  • the textbook entry
  • obvious spelling errors in the course objectives
  • the mechanics of the grading table
  • the total of the individual percentages in the grading table
We cannot alter capitalization or punctuation in the course objectives.

=

  1. Review and edit if necessary the formatting of the Course Outline and note any missing or possibly incorrect information.
  • Be sure to review the titles of each week, as well (which appear in the visual content map and in the accordion),
  • And compare them to the weekly module topics.
  • Because the visual content map is an image, note any text that needs editing in the QA Review document.
  • Edits necessary to the weekly module titles can be edited (see more in the Weekly Modules section).
If you notice a discrepancy in the titles, note it in the QA Review document so the CL director can determine the correct title.
  1. Skip Getting Started Discussion.
  2. Skip Student Lounge.
  3. Skip Student Success Survey.

=

=

Weekly Modules

  1. Review and copyedit if necessary the week's title and the first paragraph of the overview text. The checklist is reviewed separately; see step 2.

x

  1. The Edit Checklist page opens in a new tab.
  • Scroll down to navigate to the Categories and Items section.
  • Review each item:
    • To edit, click the blue text of the item (do not select the box); a new page will appear.
    • Edit the item's name and select Save.

=

  1. Briefly check the overview in each module for glaring errors. These overviews only list objectives, which cannot be edited.

=

x

Learn Submodule
  1. Review the Learn submodules' overview text, which lists the items in the Learn submodule.
  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

-

  1. Review and copyedit the Key Points pages.

;

  1. The remaining items in the Learn submodule are likely links to publisher texts and activities. Simply open the link to ensure they open properly. It's possible the links can only be opened when you login as a student.

=

=

Practice Submodule
  1. Review the Practice submodules' overview text, which lists the items in the Practice submodule.
  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

x

  1. The remaining items in the Practice submodule are likely links to publisher texts and activities. Simply open the link to ensure they open properly, or review these when you are logged in as a student.

=

=

Do Module

  1. Review the Do modules' overview text, which lists the items in the Do module.
  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

--

=

Do Submodule - Discussion
  1. Skip the overview language in the Discussion. The only text you need to review will be in the forum which is a different area.
  2. Locate the discussion link and ensure the correct week number is indicated.
  3. Click the link to the discussion forum; the forum will open in a new tab.
  • Review the discussion background and discussion questions.
  • Correct mechanical errors; revise for conciseness and clarity.
  • Document any revisions (not mechanical corrections) in the QA Review doc for transparency.
  • Recommend larger revisions in the QA Review doc.

=

=

Do Submodule - Quiz Tool Items
Graded items labeled "assignment," "assessment," "quiz," or "test" that are created in the D2L platform are housed in the Quiz tool.
  1. Verify the correct assignment name is used in step 1.
  2. Skip the boilerplate language in steps 2. - 5.

=

  1. Click on the assignment link; the assignment will open in a new tab.
  2. Copyedit as necessary the Quiz Title and the Description (if any are provided).

=

  1. Click Save and Close if edits are made or Cancel if no edits are necessary.
  2. Review any downloadable documents:
  • Ensure the correct course number is indicated in the header.
  • Review only the directions related to that specific assignment.
  • Note necessary edits in the QA Review document.
  • CL staff will edit the original document and reupload to the course.
  • Skip the rubric and the remaining boilerplate language.

=

==

Do Submodule - External Learning Tools

These items will be reviewed when you are logged into the course as a student.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ 

05/06/25

A. Lakin

articles.body:
SPECIAL NOTE

The official procedure is documented in the Clearwater Copyedit and QA Course Review Process SOP. Many areas of the course do not need review. The items that require your attention are highlighted in green.

CHECKLIST

Instructor - Course Information Module

Skip the Instructor Course Information module overview text and Instructor Guides... submodule overview text.
  • Ensure that the individual Instructor Guides match the course title (meaning, the correct guides are in the course).

____

Skip the Handout/Printed Materials... submodule overview text.
  • Review the documents within the Handout/Printed Materials submodule, which likely cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.

____

Skip the Publisher Resources module.

---

Skip the Lab Comps submodule overview text.
  • Review the Lab Comps documents. These are likely documents that cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.
Remember: The CL director does not approve lots of edits of the lab comps; only point out glaring errors.

--

Skip the remaining submodules:
  • LTI Links - Cengage submodule
  • Instructor Resources Course
  • Instructor's Guide to Ticket Submission and Teams Posts
  • Checklist (For Developers Only)

___

Student-Facing Modules

Welcome Module
Review the unique text in the Welcome module overview:
  • The first line should indicate the correct course number.
  • The course description should have been added as the first full paragraph.
  • Compare the text to the course name and description in the syllabus.
  • Stop your review at the boilerplate language beginning with "Your participation in this course..."
Skip the items within the Welcome module.

___

Getting Started - Important Information Module
Skip the Getting Started module overview text and the Course Design and Schedule submodule overview text. (NOTE: You will need to click into the Getting Started module in order to access the remainder of those items).

___

Review and edit if necessary the unique text in the Syllabus, which includes
  • the course name and number
  • the textbook entry
  • obvious spelling errors in the course objectives
  • the mechanical errors in the grading table
  • the total of the individual percentages in the grading table
We cannot alter capitalization or punctuation in the course objectives. Note glaring errors in the QA Review document.

___

Review and edit if necessary the Course Outline (only punctuation, spacing, and font style) and note any missing or possibly incorrect information.
  • Review each week's title (which appear in the visual content map and in the accordion) and edit for punctuation and spacing, if necessary.
  • Because the visual content map is an image and can't be edited in the course, note any text that needs editing in the QA Review document.
  • Compare the weekly titles in the outline to the weekly module topics (see screenshots below).
If you notice a discrepancy in the titles, note it in the QA Review document so the CL director can determine the correct title.

Skip the remaining submodules:
  • Getting Started Discussion
  • Student Lounge
  • Student Success Survey

___

Weekly Modules
Review and copyedit if necessary the week's title and the first paragraph of the week's module overview.
  • Learning objectives cannot be edited; note glaring errors (such as spelling mistakes or typos) in the QA Review document.
  • Skip the remaining boilerplate language, BUT open the checklist link.

---

The Edit Checklist page opens in a new tab.
  • Scroll down to navigate to the Categories and Items section.
  • Review each item:
    • To edit, click the blue text of the item (do not select the box); a new page will appear.
    • Edit the item's name and select Save.

x

---

Learn Submodule

  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

---

Review and copyedit the Key Points pages.

--

The remaining items in the Learn submodule are likely links to publisher texts and activities.
  • Simply open the links to ensure they open properly.
  • It's possible the links can only be opened when you login as a student.

__

Practice Submodule
Review the Practice submodules' overview text, which lists the items in the Practice submodule.
  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

___

The remaining items in the Practice submodule are likely links to publisher texts and activities.

Simply open the links to ensure they open properly, or review these when you are logged in as a student. Do not copyedit publisher materials.

___

Review the Do modules' overview text, which lists the items in the Do module.
  • Copyedit for consistency and mechanics.
  • Compare the list to the items in the course Table of Contents and note discrepancies in the QA Review document.

--

Do Submodule > Discussion

Skip the overview language in the Discussion. The only text you need to review will be in the forum which is a different area.

  • Locate the discussion link and ensure the correct week number is indicated.
  • Click the link to the discussion forum; the forum will open in a new tab.
  • Review the discussion background and discussion questions.
    • Correct mechanical errors; revise for conciseness and clarity.
    • Document any revisions (not mechanical corrections) in the QA Review doc for transparency.
    • Recommend larger revisions in the QA Review doc.

___

Do Submodule - Quiz Tool Items
  • Verify the correct assignment name is used in step 1 of the directions.
  • Skip the boilerplate language in steps 2. - 5 of the directions.

  • Click on the assignment link; the assignment will open in a new tab.
  • Copyedit as necessary the Quiz Title and the Description (if any are provided).

___

Review any downloadable documents:

  • Skip the rubric and the remaining boilerplate language.

These items will be reviewed when you are logged into the course as a student.

multilingual.body:
SPECIAL NOTE

The official procedure is documented in the Clearwater Copyedit and QA Course Review Process SOP. Many areas of the course do not need review. The items that require your attention are highlighted in green.

CHECKLIST

Instructor - Course Information Module

Skip the Instructor Course Information module overview text and Instructor Guides... submodule overview text.

____

Skip the Handout/Printed Materials... submodule overview text.
  • Review the documents within the Handout/Printed Materials submodule, which likely cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.

____

Skip the Publisher Resources module.

---

Skip the Lab Comps submodule overview text.
  • Review the Lab Comps documents. These are likely documents that cannot be edited in the course. Rather, note edits that need to be made in the QA Review document.
Remember: The CL director does not approve lots of edits of the lab comps; only point out glaring errors.

--

Skip the remaining submodules:

  • Instructor's Guide to Ticket Submission and Teams Posts

  • Stop your review at the boilerplate language beginning with "Your participation in this course..."
Skip the items within the Welcome module.

___

We only edit mechanical errors and briefly revise sentences for clarity.
  1. Once you have made your edits, click the blue Save and Close button.
If you realize you made an error or wish to discard your edits, select the Cancel button.

articles.title: Job Aid-Preparing for Content QA & EUE Review (duplicate)
articles.description: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this Job Aid is to locate documents needed to complete a Content Quality Assurance and End User Experience review of a built course. Use this Job Aid in conjunction with Contâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSEÂ . The purpose of this Job Aid is to locate documents needed to complete a Content Quality Assurance and End User Experience review of a built course. Use this Job Aid in conjunction with Contâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this Job Aid is to locate documents needed to complete a Content Quality Assurance and End User Experience review of a built course. Use this Job Aid in conjunction with Content QA & EUE Review Process SOP.

JOB AIDÂ 

I. Locate the Final Ready for Build and QA Folder

The documents you need to complete a content quality assurance and end user experience review are found in the Teams course folder called Final Ready for Build and QA. Watch this demo clip: Locating the Ready For Build Folder in Teams.mp4

I.A. Locate the Build Changes and Post Build Review Document

Any errors or issues that you find during the Content Quality Assurance and End User Experience Review will be documented in the Build Changes and Post Build Review document. Technically these aren't "changes"; however, this is where anything that has been modified after build is complete is documented.

Build Phase Change List is located in the Teams course channel in the purple folder labeled âFinal Ready for Build and QA.â

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I.B. Locate the Syllabus, Course Outline, and Instructor Resources

The syllabus, course outline, and instructor resources are all written or linked in "Build Map Zero." Locate the Word document.

The items in Build Map Zero document are not necessarily in the same order that you will see them in the course.

To confirm the instructor resources in the build map are correctly added to the D2L course, open the hidden Instructor Resources module. To see the hidden module, you must be in logged in as an admin. The eye icon with a strike-through indicates students cannot "see" this module.

The syllabus and course outline will be found in the un-hidden Getting Started module in the course.

Getting Started module: The course overview is located in the first Unit/Weekly module build map, and course syllabus and outline are found in build map "zero." Just remember to go back to Getting Started to check the course overview when you have the first Unit/Weekly module build map open.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

I.C. Locate the Build Maps

To confirm that the built course reflects the design that was created by Academics, the subject matter experts, and the CIDs, you need to compare the Build Maps with the D2L course content.

Build Maps are the modified versions of original Content Maps that have been copyedited and approved by Academics; all design comments have been deleted and student-facing text colors have been changed to black (from gold, red, pink, etc.).

The Build Maps are found in the Teams course channel in the purple folder labeled âFinal Ready for Build and QA.â

Watch this demo clip: Locate the Build Maps.mp4

II. Link Build Map to the Build Phase Change List

For documenting purposes, link each module/lesson/unit build map to the associated table in the Build Phase Change List. Watch these demo clips: Create Link of Build Map.mp4 and Link Build Map to Build Phase Change List.mp4

JOB AID LOG

Written ByÂ 

Creation Approved ByÂ

articles.title: Job Aid_Content QA and EUE
articles.description: Content Quality Assurance and End-User Experience Review. As you navigate through the course, refer to each lesson's build map to ensure each module is built correctly. Each asset should be built toâ¦
articles.short_version: Content Quality Assurance and End-User Experience Review. As you navigate through the course, refer to each lesson's build map to ensure each module is built correctly. Each asset should be built toâ¦
articles.body:
Content Quality Assurance and End-User Experience Review

  • As you navigate through the course, refer to each lesson's build map to ensure each module is built correctly. Each asset should be built to the build map specifications.
  • Assets should be listed in the D2L course in the same order as each lesson's build map (see screenshots below as an example).

  • Open all links (they should open in a new tab).
  • Open all LTIs (Cengage readings, Mindtap activities) and confirm the correct activity is linked.
LTIs: Complete the graded activities to ensure the grade transfers to the D2L gradebook. You do not need to complete the practice (ungraded) LTI activities nor read textbook assignments.

Assessments and Assignments

  • Use the answer keys (if provided) to complete assessments and quizzes.
  • Upload a test Word document for assignments that require a submission. If the assignment has a Word document template that students must download, use that document as your test Word document.
  • Post generic statements (like "EUE testing post") for the Discussion assignment.
  • Verify assessment settings noted in the build map (arrows in the first screenshot) by reviewing the Assessment Details section of the assignment before you click Start Assessment! (boxes in the second screenshot). You will see the point values of each question after you start the assessment.
