articles.title: Accessing Program Certificate of Completions
articles.description: Accessing Certificate of Completion at the end of a Program (Learner). Once the final grade has been released and a leaner meets the criteria of earning an 85% or higher in the final course, a Certifâ¦
articles.short_version: Accessing Certificate of Completion at the end of a Program (Learner). Once the final grade has been released and a leaner meets the criteria of earning an 85% or higher in the final course, a Certifâ¦
articles.body:
Accessing Certificate of Completion at the end of a Program (Learner)

Once the final grade has been released and a leaner meets the criteria of earning an 85% or higher in the final course, a Certificate of Completion will be issued.

How to Access the Certificate of Completion

Option 1: Access from Pop-Up Message

The program Certificate of Completion will automatically issue once a student has a final grade posted in their final course, earning at least an 84.5%. Awards will appear (typically) the next time a student logs in, or the next time they click the Nasium logo in the upper left side of the page to return to the main landing page.Â 

From this pop-up message, learners click the View Awards button. This will bring them to a list of all awards/badges/certificates they have earned in each course throughout the duration of the program.

To narrow down the options, learners can un-check the box on the right-hand side that reads: Include awards from other courses.

Click on the Certificate of Completion hyperlink (using Medical Assistant as an example):

Another pop-up window will appear. Here is where they can click to 'Generate Certificate' as a PDF and print out their Certificate of Completion.

The certificate will download to their browser. Next, the learner can access the download. In this example (using Microsoft Edge), the download is located in the upper right-hand corner of the browser bar.

Note: If students cannot find the download, they can use their computer's search feature to find the Download folder, containing the certificate PDF.

When students select the downloaded PDF, they can save to their computer, share the link, or print.Â 

Option 2: Access Using the Awards Icon

If the learner closes the pop-up window, or cannot locate the pop-up window, they can click on the Awards icon at the top of the Navigation bar.

Learners will be directed to this page, which will list all awards, badges, and certificates they have earned in each course throughout the duration of the program.

To narrow results, learners can un-check the box that reads: Include awards from other courses.

Click on the Certificate of Completion hyperlink (using Medical Assistant as an example):

Â 

Another pop-up window will appear. Here is where they can click to Generate Certificate as a PDF and print out their Certificate of Completion.

The certificate will download to their browser. Next, the learner can access the download. In this example (using Microsoft Edge), the download is located in the upper right-hand corner of the browser bar.

Note: If students cannot find the download, they can use their computer's search feature to find the Download folder, containing the certificate PDF.

When students select the downloaded PDF, they can save to their computer, share the link, or print.Â

articles.body:
How to Clear Cache and Cookies

When you use a browser, like Chrome, it saves some information from websites in its cache and cookies. Clearing them fixes certain problems, like loading or formatting issues on sites.

Apple Devices

For all Apple users, including those using iPhone, iPad, or Mac Laptops, using Google Chrome

Before clearing cache & cookies -

When you clear your cache and cookies, your browser might sign you out of your Google Account. To make sure you can sign back in, update your recovery phone number and email address.

In the Chrome app:

  1. On your iPhone or iPad, open the Chrome appÂ Â .
  2. At the bottom, tapÂ MoreÂ .
  3. TapÂ HistoryÂ Â  Clear browsing data.
  4. Make sure thereâs a check mark next to "Cookies, Site Data," and "Cached Images and Files."
  5. TapÂ Clear browsing data.

articles.body:
NHA Help

Here are a few video resources to help troubleshoot NHA navigation problems.

Creating an NHA Account and Using NHA Activities

NHA Tech Tips

Chat Directly with NHA

How to Create an NHA Account

  1. Locate and open the email invitation from your instructor to complete your NHA profile. This email invitation will be sent to the email address provided by your employer during Nasium registration. Follow the instructions in the email to visit the NHA website.
  1. Once on the NHA website, select the blue and white âCREATE AN ACCOUNTâ button at the bottom of the login screen.
  1. Enter the required information and select the red âCREATE AN ACCOUNTâ button at the bottom of the page. Use the same email address as you use to log into your Nasium training.
  1. Answer the demographic questions to finish setting up your account.
  1. Select the grey play button with a white triangle in the center of the screen to begin your lesson or use the Table of Contents on the far left to jump to where you left off in the lesson.
  1. Next time, just click the link inside your course to be directed to the correct learning activities.

  1. Once on the NHA website, select the blue and white âCREATE AN ACCOUNTâ button at the bottom of the login screen.
  1. Enter the required information and select the red âCREATE AN ACCOUNTâ button at the bottom of the page. Use the same email address as you use to log into your Nasium training.
  1. Answer the demographic questions to finish setting up your account.
  1. Select the grey play button with a white triangle in the center of the screen to begin your lesson or use the Table of Contents on the far left to jump to where you left off in the lesson.
  1. Next time, just click the link inside your course to be directed to the correct learning activities.

articles.title: Technology Requirements
articles.description: Hardware & Software Requirements for Nasium Learners. C omputer: Window-based computer with a 1.8 GHz frequency CPU or above Recommendation of a 2.0 GHz frequency CPU or above. Mac-based computer witâ¦
articles.short_version: Hardware & Software Requirements for Nasium Learners. C omputer: Window-based computer with a 1.8 GHz frequency CPU or above Recommendation of a 2.0 GHz frequency CPU or above. Mac-based computer witâ¦
articles.body:
Hardware & Software Requirements for Nasium Learners

Computer:

Window-basedÂ computer with a 1.8 GHz frequency CPU or above

  • Recommendation of a 2.0 GHz frequency CPU or above
  • Mac-basedÂ computer with 1.83 GHz frequency CPU or above
  • Recommendations of a 2.0 GHz frequency CPU or above

Operating System:

Windows 10

Mac OS X: 10.12 (Sierra), 10.13 (High Sierra), 10.14 (Mojave), 10.15 (Catalina) or newer

Hardware:

40 GB of free hard drive space

Ethernet connection (LAN) or wireless adapter (Wi-Fi)

RAM:

4 GB Memory (RAM) or above

Software:

Microsoft Office 2016, 2019, or 365Adobe Acrobat Reader (latest version)

Some courses may require additional software. Information on specific software is detailed within each course

Computer Screen Resolution:

1366 X 768 or higher computer screen resolution

Internet Browser:

Windows-based computers:

  • Microsoft Edge version 18 or newer
  • Mozilla Firefox (Latest version)
  • Google Chrome (Latest version)

Mac-based computers:

  • Safari Version 12 or newer
  • Mozilla Firefox (Latest version)
  • Google Chrome (Latest version)
For any browser being used, remember to Disable Pop-Up Blocker and Enable Cookies in Broswer.

To determine which Browser and Version you are using:

  • InÂ Edge, select the menu icon and choose Settings then scroll down to the bottom of the screen. The version of Edge is located below the words About this App
  • InÂ Firefox, select Firefox then âHelpâ and About Firefox. The version of Firefox is in the Version field.
  • InÂ Google Chrome, select the Customize and Control Google Chrome button on the top right-hand side of the screen then select About Google Chrome. The version of Google Chrome is in the Version field.
  • InÂ Safari, select Safari then âHelpâ and About Safari. The version of Safari is in the Version field.

Internet Service Provider:

A reliable High-Speed Internet connection is required (Upload and Download speeds of at least 5Mbps is recommended).

Audio/Visual:

Speakers

Recent video and sound card

Internal or External webcam, and microphone (recommended but not required)

articles.title: Using the Communication feature in Nasium - Learner
articles.description: Using the Communications Feature. Your homepage is equipped with the Communications feature. This option allows you to directly communicate with your Training Specialist and the Learning Support teamâ¦
articles.short_version: Using the Communications Feature. Your homepage is equipped with the Communications feature. This option allows you to directly communicate with your Training Specialist and the Learning Support teamâ¦
articles.body:
Using the Communications Feature

Your homepage is equipped with the Communications feature. This option allows you to directly communicate with your Training Specialist and the Learning Support team. There are a variety of ways to utilize this feature.

Â 

Sending a Message

Step 1: Select the Communications icon from the navigation bar at the top of the course page.

Option 1

Option 2

Step 2: Choose whether you would like to directly communicate with your Training Specialist or your Learner Support team by clicking on the associate blue hyperlink.

Â 

Step 3: Letâs use the Training Specialist Messaging Discussion as an example. Select the blue hyperlink.

Step 4: To begin a new message with your Training Specialist, select the blue Start a New Thread button.

NOTE: Be sure to enter a subject with details that can help your Training Specialist or Learner Support team understand your need, such as âQuestion about Assignment ABCâ, or âI need help accessing Assignment ABCâ. Then, type your message as you would an email.

If you would like to receive updates on your course homepage when your Training Specialist or Learner Support team responds, check the Subscribe to this thread button.
If youâd like, upload a file such as a screenshot showing details that helps to clarify your question or your ask within your message.

Step 5: Select Post to send your message.

Â 

Responding to a Message

When your Training Specialist or Learner Support team responds, you will see a notification with an orange dot from the top of the course page:

Or a red notification alert on the course tile from your main Nasium landing page.

Â 

To respond, you can select the Communications icon from within the course:

Or select the notification icon when you see a red number indicating a new message from your main Nasium landing page:

Â 

You will be directed back to the main Discussions list in your course. New messages will appear in chronological order showing the most recent.

Â 

To reply, select the blue messaging link.

Select the blue Start a New Thread button

Add a subject line, your response message, upload any documents or files if needed, and select the blue Post button to send.

Â 

Both Training Specialist and Learner Support team messaging are individual. Messages you send, as well as their response messages, are private and will only be seen by you.

Â 

It is important to note, though, that other Discussion threads such as the Lobby or any other Discussion from the actual coursework are public and can be seen by the other learners in the course. Â

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the process to create project folder in the initiation phase of a project.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. After the project is requested and approved by PMO Executive, the PM is responsible to create a project folder.
  2. Go to the Projects folder in the T-EDU Curricular Affairs.
  3. Go to the Online folder.
  4. Duplicate "Master Project Folder Structure (Rename Course + Start Date + Type of Project)" and copy this folder system into the respective course folder.
  5. Rename the folder.
  6. Share the folder with the team.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the planning session process. Planning session occurred during the planning phase to go through the Project Plan that was created by the Project Manager. This step can be considered as optional depending on the project type and the PM's familiarity on the project.

If this step is being considered as optional by the PM, please work with the Project Lead to at least confirm the appropriate tasks and timelines assigned to the project plan.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. Schedule the planning session with the Product Owner and the Project Lead after the scoping meeting.
  2. Before the planning session with the stakeholders, please create the project plan in Monday.com by following these steps from this SOP (link to the Project Plan Creation Process in Monday.com SOP - TBD).

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the project communication process through Microsoft Teams.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. Shared folder and project group chat should be created on Teams by PM after the project is onboarded.
  2. Teams group chat should be used to discuss anything project related to ensure visibility and transparency.
  3. Teams channel is used to store project document and templates.
  4. Documents are visible to stakeholders, project team members and those outside of the project, as needed, but editing permissions are given as needed.
  5. All issues or problems should be sent via email.
  6. Meetings should be set for emails that are longer than one paragraph in length.
  7. Quick questions that can be easily resolved should be sent via Teamâs chat.
  8. Requests for edits to a document should be sent via email with the link to the document provided as well as the due date.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the project plan creation process in Monday.com.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. As the Project Manager, you will have access to the Curriculum PMO Project Request Form Monday.com board where you can see all the submitted project requests.
  2. After a project request is reviewed and approved by the PMO Executive and all Curriculum verticals' LoEs have been updated, work with the PMO Executive to assign the Project Manager or the Project Lead for the project.
  3. To assign the Project Manager or the Project Lead, scroll the board to the left to find "PM/Lead Assigned" column.
  1. Then, scroll the board to the right to find the "Project Type" column. Choose the appropriate project type.
  1. An automation has been setup in this board to automatically create a board in the respective Workspace when the Project Type is chosen in this column. A notification will be sent to the assigned Project Manager/Lead.
  1. Adjust the tasks, owner, status, timeline, and due date based on the project scope and goals.
  2. Once the project plan is created and adjusted, invite the team members to the board and assign them to respective tasks.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ 

Copy Edit

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the project plan creation process in Monday.com.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. As the Project Manager, you will have access to the Curriculum PMO Project Request Form Monday.com board where you can see all the submitted project requests.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the project timeline creation process in Visio.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. In the project folder, go to "0. Project Management" folder.
  2. Select the project timeline template: 8.Template_Project Timeline
You need to have access to Visio in order to access this template.
  1. Rename the copied document with the respective project and start date.
  2. Adjust the project overall timeline. To adjust the project timeline, follow these steps:
    1. Right click on the timeline.
    2. Select "Configure Timeline".
    3. Set the dates on the "Time Period" section.
    4. Click "OK".
  3. Next, adjust the phases timelines by following these steps:
    1. Right click on each of the phase.
    2. Select "Configure Interval".
    3. Set the "Start Date" and the "Finish Date" based on the project plan.
    4. Click "OK".
  4. Lastly, adjust the milestone dates by following these steps:
    1. Right click on each milestone.
    2. Select "Configure Milestone".
    3. Set the "Milestone Date" based on the project plan.
    4. Click "OK".
  5. Share this project timeline with the project team and stakeholders.

articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the risk assessment process during the scoping meeting and how to track risk throughout the life cycle of the project.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Risk Assessment

  1. Risk assessment started when the project requestor fills in the project request form. In the form, the project requestor is asked to provide 3-5 or more business, operational, or students/learners risks that can occur in the project.
  2. Transfer those risks into the scope document when draft the document.
  3. Discuss the risks and their mitigation plans with the project requestor and the Project Lead during the scoping meeting.
  4. Discuss the level of probability and impact of these risks.
This is a crucial step in risk assessment to identify the risk's level of severity.
  1. Once the scope document is signed, transfer those risk to the Risk Register - Curriculum Monday board.

Registering Risks into Risk Register in Monday.com

  1. Go to the Curriculum Workspace.
  2. Go to the Risk Register - Curriculum Monday board.
  3. Add new group and rename the group with the project name.
  4. Transfer the risks that are discussed in the scoping meeting to the Risk column. Write the Description and the Implications of the Risks.
  5. Assign the respective Owners to the Risk.
For technical risks, assign the respective Curriculum managers or leads to mitigate those risks. For timeline, scope, and stakeholders related risks, assign the PM to mitigate those risks.
  1. Update the "Probability" and "Impact" levels based on the team's and your assessment.
No need to modify the "Risk Level" as Monday.com automation will automatically assign the risk level based on the Probability and Impact levels that are assigned.
  1. Next, update the "Risk Response" column based on the agreed upon planned resolution.
  2. Lastly, transfer the planned resolutions that were discussed during the scoping meeting to the "Planned Resolution" column.

Tracking Risks during Project

  1. When there are new risks that arise in the project, document those risks in the Risk Register. Follow the above steps to document the new risks.
  2. When there are risks that materialized or active, go to the Risk Register and change the "Risk Status" column.

SOP LOG

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the process occurred during the scoping and planning meeting.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. Prior to this meeting, PM should fill in some information in the scope document, create a project plan in Monday.com, and mockup the project timeline. Refer to these SOPs on the processes mentioned above:

Note to SOP writer - need to link these SOPs to the appropriate articles.

    5. Risks
    6. Success Factor

The purpose of this SOP is to document the process to manage the project's stakeholders through the utilization of a RACI chart.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the daily check-in meeting or daily stand-up meeting that is implemented in a rapid course development project.

PROCEDURE

  1. Daily check-in meeting should be scheduled before the project starts. Check-in meeting should occur at the same time from Monday-Thursday (Friday is
  2. The structure of this meeting will follow these 3 guiding questions:
    1. What did you do yesterday?
    2. What will you do today?
    3. Is there anything that blocking your progress?
  3. During this check-in meeting, it is the PM responsibility to update the project plan if the task status is not updated.
  4. Send out the meeting notes through email after the meeting.
  5. Upload the meeting notes to the project board in the "Conversation" section of "Monitor project progress".

For Create/Build Phase, the check-in meeting frequency will be reduced to only 2 times per week. Please adjust the meeting accordingly.

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the kick-off process in the course development project.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Design Phase Kick-Off

  1. This kick-off usually after all PMO documentations (scope document, project plan, and project timeline) are finalized and before the design planning phase starts.
  2. Stakeholders who need to be invited in this meeting are:
    1. Program Leader
    2. Project Lead
    3. SMEs
    4. CID
    5. Copy Editor/QA
  3. In the project folder, go to "0. Project Management" folder.
  4. Select "9.Project Kick-Off Presentation_Name_Date".
  5. Fill in the slide deck with the information regarding the project's design phase.
  6. At the end of the kick-off meeting, share this slide deck with the meeting participant.

Create/Build Phase Kick-Off

  1. This kick-off happens after the course blueprint is approved by the Program Leader and design phase is ready to start.
  2. Stakeholders who need to be invited in this meeting are:
    1. Project Lead
    2. CID
    3. MM manager and MM senior
    4. ITS manager and ITS team member assigned to the project
  3. Before the meeting, work with the CID to add the ITS and MM high-level overview scope to the slide deck that was used for design kick-off.
  4. At the end of the kick-off meeting, share this slide deck with the meeting participant.

Build Maps Review Meeting

  1. This meeting happens after all build maps are reviewed and approved by ITS and MM managers. The purpose of this meeting is to provide an opportunity for the ITS and MM teams to ask the CID assigned to the project of any clarifying questions prior to them starting the tasks.
  2. Similar stakeholders from the Create/Build Phase Kick-Off should be invited to this meeting.

SOP LOG

SOP Creation DateÂ 

Written ByÂ

multilingual.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the kick-off process in the course development project.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Design Phase Kick-Off

  1. This kick-off usually after all PMO documentations (scope document, project plan, and project timeline) are finalized and before the design planning phase starts.
  2. Stakeholders who need to be invited in this meeting are:
    1. Program Leader
    2. Project Lead
    3. SMEs
    4. CID
    5. Copy Editor/QA
  3. In the project folder, go to "0. Project Management" folder.
  4. Select "9.Project Kick-Off Presentation_Name_Date".
  5. Fill in the slide deck with the information regarding the project's design phase.
  6. At the end of the kick-off meeting, share this slide deck with the meeting participant.

Create/Build Phase Kick-Off

  1. This kick-off happens after the course blueprint is approved by the Program Leader and design phase is ready to start.
  2. Stakeholders who need to be invited in this meeting are:
    1. Project Lead
    2. CID
    3. MM manager and MM senior
    4. ITS manager and ITS team member assigned to the project
  3. Before the meeting, work with the CID to add the ITS and MM high-level overview scope to the slide deck that was used for design kick-off.
  4. At the end of the kick-off meeting, share this slide deck with the meeting participant.

Build Maps Review Meeting

  1. This meeting happens after all build maps are reviewed and approved by ITS and MM managers. The purpose of this meeting is to provide an opportunity for the ITS and MM teams to ask the CID assigned to the project of any clarifying questions prior to them starting the tasks.
  2. Similar stakeholders from the Create/Build Phase Kick-Off should be invited to this meeting.

SOP LOG

articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the process to maintain the ONL/CLW General Reporting Dashboard in Monday.com. This dashboard serves as a main project progress communication for external stakeholders.

PROCEDUREÂ 

Adding New Project to the Dashboard

  1. When there is a new project in the Curriculum PMO portfolio and a project plan is created, add the new project to the ONL/CLW Curriculum Reporting Dashboard.
  2. To add or connect the board, follow these steps:
    1. Click "Connected Boards" on the top left side of the dashboard.
    1. Search the board by name.
    2. Check the checkbox in the front of the board name and click Done.

Adjust Widgets Settings

  1. After the new board is connected, the widgets settings need to be changed. Follow these steps to adjust widgets settings:
Adjust the "Ticket Request Types" Setting
  1. Click the three dots on the right-hand corner of the widget.
  2. Click "Settings".
  1. Open the "Boards" fold.
  2. Uncheck the new board that is connected to this widget.
Adjust the "Ticket Status per Department" Setting
  1. Click the three dots on the right-hand corner of the widget.
  2. Click "Settings".
  1. Open the "Boards" fold.
  2. Uncheck the new board that is connected to this widget.

SOP LOG

PURPOSEÂ 

The purpose of this SOP is to document the process to submit PTO to the Project Manager. Curriculum PMO uses Monday.com to plan out the Curriculum department resources. In order to reflect each resource's hours effectively, Curriculum team members should inform the Curriculum PM their PTO dates.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. After PTO dates are approved by managers through UltiPro, inform the Curriculum PM of the planned PTO.
  2. Curriculum PM: Inquire team's PTO during the project's kick-off meeting.
  3. Document the PTO dates in the Billable Hours group in 2024 Curriculum Resource Planning Board.
  4. Find the team member's name and click to open the subitems.
  5. Type "PTO" and put it the dates, timelines, and estimated hours allocated for that planned PTO.

articles.short_version: PURPOSE. Walk through the steps required to put a new lead/learner/student FOR NASIUM into Anthology. PROCEDURE. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Sesâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

Walk through the steps required to put a new lead/learner/student FOR NASIUM into Anthology.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology
  2. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Session Defaults. Search for and set your Session Defaults to "NASTRN" and the correct Award Year. Once saved, you should be directed to the home page.
  3. From the home page, click the blue "Student" tile towards the top left.
  4. Once on the Student main page, click "+ New Student" on the top left-center of the page.
  5. Fill in as much information as you have available, but at minimum, First and Last Name (required).
  6. Minimize the Personal Information drop down and expand the Recruitment Information drop down options.
  7. Once the Recruitment Information tab is expanded, fill in at least the required (*) items; Campus, School Status, Lead Source, Lead Date, and Adm. Rep.
    1. Campus should already be filled in with: Nasium Training
    2. School status should already be filled in with: New Lead.
    3. Lead Date (start) should already be filled in with the date the Lead was created (today).
  1. Use the drop-down arrow to select the correct Lead Source.
  1. Use the drop-down arrow to select the correct Admin Rep. If there was not an Admin Rep who a input this lead, select .Unassigned
  2. Under Program, select the correct program that this new student is a lead for.
  3. Hit Save and you'll return to the main Student home page. Now your lead/learner/student will have been created within Anthology.

From the Student home page, find your new lead/learner/student and validate that their information was saved correctly. Once you open the student's information, the student's SYID will now be visible in the URL.

articles.description: PURPOSE. Walk through the steps required to put a new lead/learner/student FOR NASIUM into Anthology. PROCEDURE. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Sesâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSE. Walk through the steps required to put a new lead/learner/student FOR NASIUM into Anthology. PROCEDURE. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Sesâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSEÂ 

Walk through the steps required to enroll and register learner/student FOR NASIUM into Anthology.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology
  2. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Session Defaults. Search for and set your Session Defaults to "NASTRN" and the correct Award Year. Once saved, you should be directed to the home page.
  3. From the home page, click the blue "Student" tile towards the top left.
  4. Once on the Student main page, in "Student Search" add Student Name or SysID. Located at the top center of the page.
  5. Confirm Learner "Personal Information" First Name, Last Name, SSN (first 9 digits of learner phone number), and Date of Birth.
    1. Required Personal may change as required by partner, state or certifying entity.
  6. Confirm "Contact Information" Telephone, Mobile Number, e-Mail, Other email (Learner Nasium email address), Address, City, State.
  7. Confirm "Recruitment Information" Campus, School Status, Lead Source, Adm. Rep, Program.
  8. Scroll to the top of the page, right side. Click "Academic Records", then click "Enrollments".
  9. Select "New".
    To enroll you will complete sections for New Enrollment, Program Information, Application Responses to complete enrollment
  10. 1. New Enrollment - Click "Next".
  11. 2. "Program Information" - in this section complete 8 fields: Program Type, Student Staus, Program, Program Version, Version Start Date, Grade Level, Term & Billing Method, Billing Method. Then Click "Next".
    1. Program Type - Select Non-Degree from the Dropdown Menu.
    2. Student Status - from the Dropdown Menu select NDS - Enrollment.
    3. Program - Select appropriate program from the Dropdown Menu.
    4. Program Version - Select HOPL - Only Pre-Licensing Course from the Dropdown Menu
    5. Version Start Date - Select appropriate cohort from Dropdown Menu
    6. Grade Level - Select 1st year, attended before or any other from the Dropdown Menu
    7. Term & Billing Method - Click Dropbox Menu under Start Term, Select appropriate cohort.
    8. Billing Method - Select NT4BM Non-Title Billing Method from Dropdown Menu
    9. Click "Next"
  12. 3. Enrollment Information - Click Next"
  1. 4. Application Responses - Click "Enroll"
  2. Enrollments page will populate displaying learners active program enrollment information.

Learner program enrollment is complete, next steps for Registration.

  1. After learner enrollment is complete, click "Academic Records" on right side of screen. Click "Student Courses".
  2. Courses will populate, select appropriate course for enrollment.
  3. After selecting the course, click the "Register" bottom.
  4. Scroll down to 1. Course Information
    1. From the Term tab, select the cohort term from the Dropdown Menu; then click Next
  5. Scroll down to 2. Class Section Selection
    1. Click course to highlight cohort start and end dates will populate, scroll down and click, Register.
  1. Course registration completed; Student Courses field will update reflecting the active course registration.

PURPOSEÂ 

Walk through the steps required to Batch Enroll learners FOR NASIUM into Anthology.

PROCEDUREÂ 

  1. 1. Log into Anthology: UMA Anthology
  2. Upon sign on, you'll be required to set your Session Defaults. Search for and set your Session Defaults to "NASTRN" and the correct Award Year. Once saved, you should be directed to the home page.
  3. From the home page, click the blue "Processes" tile towards the top left.
  4. Once on the Processes main page, click "Registration" on the top left-center of the page.
  5. From 1. Term select Term
  6. From the pop out window Select Term scroll through the cohort's listed
    1. Click the box to select appropriate cohort; then click "Select".
  7. Once the Term information updates, the course will populate under Select a Class Section offered at Nasium Training.
    1. Select course and click "Next".
  8. Under 2. Results - Students to Register. You will find the list of the learners registered in the Class Section selected in, Step 7.
    1. Student Group, Registration Group, Shift information should automatically populate.
    2. Then click "Student" to select all learners registered in course or select individual students.
    3. From the popout window Submit Batch, click "Quene".

â

  1. Once you click Quene the group is registered in course. See confirmation message in top right hand of page.

articles.title: ITS - Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, create Excel file, then Word file in ChatGPTÂ - Not Respondus
articles.description: PURPOSE. The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting. PROCEDURE. Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, createâ¦
articles.short_version: PURPOSE. The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting. PROCEDURE. Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, createâ¦
articles.body:
PURPOSE

The purpose of this job aid is to document the process for preparing an assignment or assessment in the LMS test tool using Respondus formatting.

PROCEDURE

Extract Quiz Pool in D2L, create Excel file, then Word file in ChatGPT

Step A: Extract Quiz Pool from D2L.

Brightspace Native Export (Brightspace Package / XML)Â 

You can use Brightspaceâs built-inÂ Import/Export/Copy ComponentsÂ tool to export quiz/question content.Â 

Â 

  1. Go toÂ Course Admin â Import/Export/Copy Components.Â 

Â Â 

  1. ChooseÂ Export as Brightspace Package.Â Then click Start.

Â 

  1. SelectÂ Question LibraryÂ (or Quizzes) and select "Select individual items to export". Then click continue.

Â Â 

  1. Check the box of the assessment you want to extract.Â ClickÂ Continue.Â 

Â Â 

  1. Do not check the box. ClickÂ Export.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Export started. Click on Done.
    You may have to wait until you see aÂ notification in the "bell"Â icon above.Â (SeeÂ nextÂ 2 screenshots.)Â Â 

You may have to wait a few minutesÂ before the red dot appearsÂ byÂ the bell icon.Â Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Click on the bell icon.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. ClickÂ on the blue link with the assessment name you are extracting.Â 

Â 

Â 

  1. Download the resulting ZIP file by clicking on the blue link.Â Â 

Â 

  1. It willÂ downloadÂ as a ZIP file. Keep itÂ asÂ is.Â Â 

This gives youÂ IMS QTI/XML filesÂ containingÂ questions that other tools or converters can read.Â Â 

Â 

Step B: Ask ChatGPT extract the file into an Excel file.Â 

  1. Go to ChatGPT.Â 
  2. Start a newÂ chat in ChatGPT and attach your Zip fileÂ with the + (plus) sign on the ChatGPT text bubble.Â 

Â Â Â Â Â Â (disregardÂ the text in the bubble above.)Â Â 

  1. Use this prompt (all of these sentences):Â Â 

IÂ have exported a zip file usingÂ Brightspace Native Export (Brightspace Package / XML).Â Â 

PleaseÂ unnzipÂ Brightspace export packageÂ andÂ convert this file to text in an MS Excel document.Â 

Target theÂ QTI assessmentÂ Item structureÂ that D2L usesÂ and create anÂ Excel file.Â 

CreateÂ theÂ ExcelÂ fileÂ using the D2L/QTI 1.x structureÂ ifÂ necessaryÂ so the content appears in the Excel file.Â 

PreserveÂ all original wording.Â 

Â 

  1. After waiting a few minutes, you should get an Excel file that looks like this, depending on how many questions itÂ contains.
    Download it and adjust the columns.Â 

Â 

  1. After the first one, you can use this prompt and attach the next zipped assessment file.Â 

Â (Note that it may not download matching question text correctly.)

  1. Download the file.
  2. You can rename the file to an appropriate course name like SC1000_W2L3_Assessment_Pool_1 .

Step C: Convert Excel sheet to MS Word file

1. Create a new chat in ChatGPT.

2. Copy this prompt (all of these sentences) into ChatGPT. -- Also: Attach the Excel file.

I exported a question bank from D2L Brightspace from the question library and then had ChatGPT convert it to an Excel spreadsheet file.

Can you convert this Excel spreadsheet file into an MS Word file (with the questions, answers, and correct answers indicated) so it is formatted so it can be added to Respondus test question generating software, with proper formatting for Respondus.

Do not change any wording in the file.

Please ignore any extra information in this Excel file that is not needed to generate the questions and answers with correct answers noted.

3. After using the prompt once, you can prompt, "Do the same thing with this file." with the next file you want to convert to an MS Word file.

4. Rename the file to the correct name.

5. Check that the questions match the course questions.
